one_line_ask: Clean and de-skew the client's three AI-generated tea-brand raster logos, trace them into crisp scalable SVG masters, derive one-color and halftone print variants of the crest, and produce a matching front-facing stand-up-pouch label base panel from the supplied pouch mockup.
brief: Amberleaf Tea Studio, a small independent tea-branding consultancy, is prepping two of its house tea brands for a production run and needs the artwork made print-ready. The studio has already generated the concept artwork with an AI image tool but only has flat raster PNGs: a wordmark lockup for 'Golden Chai Co.' (warm amber-and-cream with a subtle gold gradient), a wordmark lockup for 'Silverleaf Green' (cool sage-green with a fine leaf glyph), and one ornamental 'crest' mark (a symmetrical scrollwork badge with a small tea-leaf motif meant to sit on both brands' packaging). All three rasters have the usual AI-export problems: soft anti-aliased edges, a faint off-white textured card behind the art, mild luminance noise, and each is tilted a couple of degrees off level. None of these can go to a printer as-is because they do not scale and have no transparency. The studio also supplies a flat mockup image of a blank stand-up coffee/tea pouch it wants to use as the packaging shape. The job: (1) ingest all three raster logos and get clean working copies; auto-straighten each tilted mark and tonally clean it so edges are true and the near-white card is even; (2) crop each mark tight to
